# ntui

> An Ink-style TUI library for Rust: components as functions + hooks, an
> `element!` JSX-like macro, a React-style keyed reconciler over a retained
> fiber tree, taffy flexbox layout, cell-diff painting, and both fullscreen
> and inline crossterm backends on a tokio event loop.

ntui lets Rust developers build terminal UIs the way Ink lets JavaScript
developers build them: `#[component]` functions call hooks (`use_state`,
`use_effect`, `use_input`, `use_future`, `use_stream`, `use_context`,
`use_terminal_size`, `use_scroll`, `use_scrollback`, `use_app`) and return an
`element!` tree of `View`/`Text` nodes. State changes trigger re-renders; the
engine reconciles the tree, computes flexbox layout, paints to an in-memory
cell buffer, diffs against the previous frame, and writes only the changed
cells to the terminal.

- License: MIT OR Apache-2.0 (dual-licensed, user's choice)
- Language: Rust, 2024 edition
- Install: `ntui = "0.1"` in Cargo.toml
- Repository: https://github.com/quinnjr/ntui

## Docs

- [Getting Started](https://quinnjr.github.io/ntui/docs/getting-started): install, first component, the render loop, testing without a TTY.
- [Hooks Reference](https://quinnjr.github.io/ntui/docs/hooks): every hook's signature and when to use it.
- [Architecture](https://quinnjr.github.io/ntui/docs/architecture): the fiber tree, reconciler, layout, paint pipeline, and the fullscreen vs. inline rendering backends.

## Notes for automated agents

- v1 limitations: no mouse support (keyboard input only), text is measured at
  1 column per `char` (not grapheme/display width — wide/combining
  characters can misalign), and `use_context` consumers that skip
  re-rendering may observe a stale context value until something else marks
  them dirty.
- Not a widget library: ntui does not ship pre-built widgets (lists, tables,
  progress bars); the flexbox `View`/`Text` primitives plus hooks are the
  whole surface.
